paper_title: Xanthomonas campestris pv. vesicatoria Secretes Proteases and Xylanases via the Xps Type II Secretion System and Outer Membrane Vesicles
verbatim_quote: Reduced amounts of XCV0536, the putative protease XCV3671, and the predicted xylanases XCV4358 and XCV4360 were present in the culture supernatant of the T2S mutant strain compared with the wild type, suggesting that the efficient secretion of these proteins depends on the T2S systems.
note: stored DOI was actually valid; audit 404 was a different accession

paper_title: Coxiella burnetii inhibits host immunity by a protein phosphatase adapted from glycolysis
verbatim_quote: Biogenesis of this compartment requires effectors of its Dot/Icm type IV secretion system... we identified the effector Cbu0513 (designated as CinF) as an inhibitor of NF-kappaB signaling.
note: LOCUS FLAG: paper designates CinF as Cbu0513; corpus locus_tag is CBU0041. Verify pass must reconcile.

paper_title: Coxiella burnetii effector protein CvpF subverts RAB26-dependent autophagy to promote vacuole biogenesis and virulence
verbatim_quote: we demonstrate that CBU0626 is a novel member of the Coxiella vacuolar protein (Cvp) family of effector proteins, which is translocated by the Dot/Icm secretion system.
note: renamed CvpF; locus CBU0626 matches corpus

paper_title: The Brucella TIR domain containing proteins BtpA and BtpB have a structural WxxxE motif...
verbatim_quote: The TIR domain-containing proteins BtpA/Btp1/TcpB and BtpB are translocated into host cells by the facultative intracellular bacterial pathogen Brucella.
note: Teo 2026-06-10: KEEP as T4SS. Host translocation confirmed (Felix 2014); Brucella's only host-effector route is VirB T4SS. ROUTE-UNCERTAIN like Sca4: host translocation confirmed, but Myeni 2013 VirB screen (10.1371/journal.ppat.1003556) confirmed only BspA-F, not BtpA/TcpB. Brucella-only-VirB argues keep. Awaiting Teo.

paper_title: The Brucella TIR domain containing proteins BtpA and BtpB have a structural WxxxE motif...
verbatim_quote: The TIR domain-containing proteins BtpA/Btp1/TcpB and BtpB are translocated into host cells by the facultative intracellular bacterial pathogen Brucella.
note: Teo 2026-06-10: KEEP as T4SS. Host translocation confirmed (Felix 2014); Brucella's only host-effector route is VirB T4SS. B. abortus paralog of TcpB_Btp1; same route-uncertainty. Awaiting Teo.
